title: AOI analysis workflow
text: vignette shows practical AOI workflow Gazepoint GP3 / Gazepoint Analysis exports using gp3tools. workflow covers: creating validating master table; checking AOI geometry coding; creating AOI-entry AOI-window summaries; fitting AOI-window GLMMs; extracting fixation, AOI, transition features; fitting AOI time-course GAMMs; running model diagnostics reporting checks. vignette chunks evaluated package checks, file can serve readable workflow template. first section uses built-example data; optional import chunk shows replace objects data private Gazepoint export folder. adapting code, replace placeholder paths, AOI labels, time windows, column names used study.

what: 1. Load example data or import your own exports
title: AOI analysis workflow
text: quick read-, start lightweight synthetic example data included package. objects make later workflow easier follow already use column names expected examples. real study, replace example objects importing Gazepoint export folder. optional chunk creates all_gaze all_fix, rebuilds master imported gaze rows:
code: library(gp3tools)  data("gazepoint_example_master") data("gazepoint_example_fixations") data("gazepoint_example_aoi_geometry") data("gazepoint_example_aoi_windows")  master <- gazepoint_example_master all_fix <- gazepoint_example_fixations aoi_geometry <- gazepoint_example_aoi_geometry aoi_windows <- gazepoint_example_aoi_windows export_dir <- "C:/Users/YourName/Desktop/gp3_test_exports" output_dir <- "C:/Users/YourName/Desktop/gp3_outputs"  results <- run_gazepoint_workflow(   export_dir = export_dir,   output_dir = output_dir,   prefix = "study1",   save_plots = TRUE,   create_report = TRUE )  all_gaze <- results$all_gaze all_fix <- results$all_fix master <- create_gazepoint_master(all_gaze)

title: AOI analysis workflow
text: Use validation output continuing AOI coding, AOI-window summaries, model fitting. imported real Gazepoint exports, point confirm expected participant, trial, time, gaze, fixation, AOI-related columns created correctly.
code: master_audit <- audit_gazepoint_master(master)  validation <- validate_gazepoint_master(master)  master_audit$overview validation$summary validation$checks

title: AOI analysis workflow
text: example data already includes aoi_geometry object. real study, create import geometry table one row per AOI stimulus. minimal geometry template looks like : Audit AOI geometry: Audit AOI overlap: Validate observed AOI labels geometry-derived labels: Create visual AOI verification plot:
code: aoi_geometry_template <- tibble::tibble(   media_id = c("stim1", "stim1"),   aoi = c("logo", "product"),   x_min = c(0.10, 0.50),   y_min = c(0.10, 0.50),   x_max = c(0.30, 0.70),   y_max = c(0.30, 0.70) )  aoi_geometry_template aoi_geometry_audit <- audit_gazepoint_aoi_geometry(   aoi_geometry,   aoi_col = "aoi",   stimulus_col = "media_id",   x_min_col = "x_min",   y_min_col = "y_min",   x_max_col = "x_max",   y_max_col = "y_max",   screen_x_range = c(0, 1),   screen_y_range = c(0, 1) )  aoi_geometry_audit$overview aoi_geometry_audit$flagged_aois aoi_overlap_audit <- audit_gazepoint_aoi_overlap(   aoi_geometry,   aoi_col = "aoi",   stimulus_col = "media_id",   x_min_col = "x_min",   y_min_col = "y_min",   x_max_col = "x_max",   y_max_col = "y_max",   min_overlap_area = 0,   min_overlap_prop = 0 )  aoi_overlap_audit$overview aoi_overlap_audit$flagged_overlaps aoi_coding_audit <- audit_gazepoint_aoi_coding_matrix(   gaze_data = master,   aoi_geometry = aoi_geometry,   observed_aoi_col = "aoi_current",   gaze_x_col = "x",   gaze_y_col = "y",   gaze_stimulus_col = "MEDIA_ID",   sample_id_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   geometry_aoi_col = "aoi",   geometry_stimulus_col = "media_id",   x_min_col = "x_min",   y_min_col = "y_min",   x_max_col = "x_max",   y_max_col = "y_max" )  aoi_coding_audit$overview aoi_coding_audit$coding_matrix aoi_verification_plot <- plot_gazepoint_aoi_verification(   aoi_geometry = aoi_geometry,   gaze_data = master,   geometry_aoi_col = "aoi",   geometry_stimulus_col = "media_id",   x_min_col = "x_min",   y_min_col = "y_min",   x_max_col = "x_max",   y_max_col = "y_max",   gaze_x_col = "x",   gaze_y_col = "y",   gaze_stimulus_col = "MEDIA_ID" )  aoi_verification_plot

title: AOI analysis workflow
text: examples assume gaze sample already assigned AOI-state column called aoi_current. data, check AOI labels used target_aoi_values distractor_aoi_values match coding scheme. Create ordered AOI-entry episodes: Create AOI-window summaries: Inspect target-looking proportions:
code: aoi_entries <- summarise_gazepoint_aoi_entries(   master,   time_col = "time",   aoi_col = "aoi_current",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global") )  dplyr::glimpse(aoi_entries) aoi_windows <- summarise_gazepoint_aoi_windows(   master,   windows = c(0, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000, 10000),   time_col = "time",   aoi_col = "aoi_current",   subject_col = "subject",   condition_col = "condition",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   target_aoi_values = "AOI 2",   distractor_aoi_values = c("AOI 0", "AOI 1") )  dplyr::glimpse(aoi_windows) dplyr::select(   aoi_windows,   subject,   condition,   MEDIA_ID,   trial_global,   window_label,   n_window_samples,   n_target_samples,   n_distractor_samples,   n_valid_denominator_samples,   target_sample_prop_valid,   aoi_window_status )

title: AOI analysis workflow
text: Use AOI-window GLMM predefined confirmatory windows. fitting model, check AOI-window denominators sparse imbalanced windows visible. Audit denominator adequacy: Prepare binomial success/failure data: Fit main AOI-window GLMM: Transform AOI proportions empirical logits sensitivity models: Run model-family sensitivity checks:
code: aoi_window_denominator_audit <- audit_gazepoint_aoi_window_denominators(   aoi_windows,   min_denominator_samples = 5,   min_valid_denominator_prop = 0.70,   max_denominator_cv = 0.25,   max_condition_ratio = 2 )  aoi_window_denominator_audit$overview aoi_window_denominator_audit$window_summary aoi_window_denominator_audit$condition_window_summary aoi_glmm_data <- prepare_gazepoint_aoi_glmm_data(   aoi_windows,   success_col = "n_target_samples",   denominator = "valid",   subject_col = "subject",   condition_col = "condition",   window_col = "window_label",   window_start_col = "window_start_ms",   window_end_col = "window_end_ms",   min_denominator_samples = 5,   outcome_label = "target" )  dplyr::count(   aoi_glmm_data,   aoi_glmm_condition,   aoi_glmm_window,   aoi_glmm_status ) aoi_glmm_fit <- fit_gazepoint_aoi_window_glmm(   aoi_glmm_data,   random_window_slopes = FALSE )  aoi_glmm_fit$model_status aoi_glmm_fit$formula aoi_glmm_fit$comparison aoi_glmm_fit$fixed_effects aoi_emp_logit <- transform_gazepoint_aoi_empirical_logit(   aoi_glmm_data,   numerator_col = "aoi_glmm_success",   denominator_col = "aoi_glmm_denominator",   correction = 0.5 )  attr(aoi_emp_logit, "gp3_empirical_logit_overview") aoi_sensitivity <- fit_gazepoint_aoi_model_sensitivity(   aoi_glmm_data,   model_types = c(     "binomial_glmm",     "empirical_logit_lmm",     "proportion_lmm",     "quasibinomial_glm"   ),   include_condition = TRUE,   include_window = TRUE,   include_interaction = TRUE,   random_intercept = TRUE )  aoi_sensitivity$comparison aoi_sensitivity$fixed_effects

title: AOI analysis workflow
text: next helpers create descriptive trial-level sequence-level features. need every feature every study; select summaries match research question. Create transition-ready AOI sequences: Summarise AOI transitions: Create transition matrices: Create time-varying transition matrices: Create trial-level AOI features: Create trial-level fixation features Gazepoint fixation exports: Create fixation-, saccade-, AOI-contingent aligned data:
code: aoi_sequences <- prepare_gazepoint_aoi_sequences(   master,   time_col = "time",   aoi_col = "aoi_current",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global") )  dplyr::glimpse(aoi_sequences) aoi_transition_summary <- summarise_gazepoint_aoi_transitions(   master,   time_col = "time",   aoi_col = "aoi_current",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   target_aoi_values = "AOI 2",   distractor_aoi_values = c("AOI 0", "AOI 1") )  aoi_transition_summary aoi_transition_matrix <- compute_gazepoint_aoi_transition_matrix(   master,   time_col = "time",   aoi_col = "aoi_current",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   target_aoi_values = "AOI 2",   distractor_aoi_values = c("AOI 0", "AOI 1") )  aoi_transition_matrix$count_matrix aoi_transition_matrix$probability_matrix aoi_transition_matrix$long_table time_varying_transition_matrix <- compute_gazepoint_time_varying_transition_matrix(   master,   time_col = "time",   state_col = "aoi_current",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   windows = c(0, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000) )  time_varying_transition_matrix$overview time_varying_transition_matrix$transition_summary aoi_trial_features <- summarise_gazepoint_aoi_trial_features(   master,   time_col = "time",   aoi_col = "aoi_current",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   target_aoi_values = "AOI 2",   distractor_aoi_values = c("AOI 0", "AOI 1") )  aoi_trial_features fixation_trial_features <- summarise_gazepoint_fixation_trials(   all_fix,   target_aoi_values = "AOI 2",   distractor_aoi_values = c("AOI 0", "AOI 1") )  fixation_trial_features fixation_aligned <- prepare_gazepoint_fixation_aligned_data(   master,   time_col = "time",   participant_col = "subject",   trial_col = "trial_global",   aoi_col = "aoi_current",   target_aoi = "AOI 2",   fixation_col = "is_fixation",   alignment_event = "first_fixation_to_target",   baseline_window = c(-200, 0),   analysis_window = c(0, 800) )  fixation_aligned$overview fixation_aligned$event_table fixation_aligned$trial_summary

title: AOI analysis workflow
text: preparation helpers optional. Use plan Markov-chain, semi-Markov, HMM-style sequence analyses; otherwise, section can skipped. Prepare dependency-free sequence-model objects: helpers prepare sequence transition data inspection, reporting, export, specialist modelling packages. directly fit Markov-chain, semi-Markov, hidden Markov models.
code: markov_obj <- create_gazepoint_markovchain_object(   aoi_sequences,   state_col = "aoi" )  semimarkov_data <- prepare_gazepoint_semimarkov_data(   aoi_sequences,   state_col = "aoi",   time_col = "time" )  hmm_data <- prepare_gazepoint_hmm_data(   aoi_sequences,   state_col = "aoi",   observation_cols = c("x", "y", "pupil") )  markov_obj$overview semimarkov_data$overview hmm_data$overview

title: AOI analysis workflow
text: Prepare AOI-GAMM data AOI-state column: Fit AOI time-course GAMM: Plot observed fitted AOI trajectories: AOI time-course GAMMs complementary AOI-window GLMMs. Use AOI-window GLMMs predefined confirmatory windows AOI-GAMMs research question concerns smooth target-looking trajectories.
code: aoi_gamm_data <- prepare_gazepoint_aoi_gamm_data(   master,   aoi_col = "aoi_current",   target_aoi_values = "AOI 2",   subject_col = "subject",   condition_col = "condition",   time_col = "time",   trial_col = "trial_global",   time_window = c(0, 2000),   bin_size_ms = 50,   denominator = "valid",   min_denominator_samples = 1,   outcome_label = "target_aoi" )  dplyr::count(   aoi_gamm_data,   .gp3_aoi_gamm_condition,   .gp3_aoi_gamm_condition_status,   .gp3_aoi_gamm_status ) aoi_gamm_fit <- fit_gazepoint_aoi_gamm(   aoi_gamm_data,   include_condition = TRUE,   condition_smooths = TRUE,   random_subject = TRUE,   random_subject_time = FALSE,   time_k = 10 )  aoi_gamm_fit$model_status aoi_gamm_fit$condition_status aoi_gamm_fit$formula_text aoi_gamm_fit$diagnostics aoi_gamm_fit$smooth_table aoi_gamm_plot <- plot_gazepoint_aoi_gamm(   aoi_gamm_fit,   n_time_points = 100,   include_observed = TRUE,   include_fitted = TRUE,   show_ci = TRUE )  aoi_gamm_plot

title: AOI analysis workflow
text: Finish checking fitted model collecting reporting-ready summaries. outputs intended support manuscript reporting rather replace statistical interpretation. Run model diagnostics: Create model-summary tables: Estimate marginal means emmeans installed: Export manuscript-ready tables: Create reporting checklist:
code: aoi_diagnostics <- diagnose_gazepoint_glmm(   aoi_glmm_fit,   model_name = "aoi_window_glmm",   use_dharma = FALSE )  aoi_diagnostics$overview aoi_diagnostics$convergence aoi_diagnostics$singularity aoi_diagnostics$overdispersion aoi_model_summary <- tidy_gazepoint_model_summary(   aoi_glmm_fit,   model_name = "aoi_window_glmm",   exponentiate = TRUE,   use_dharma = FALSE )  aoi_model_summary$overview aoi_model_summary$fixed_effects aoi_emmeans <- summarise_gazepoint_emmeans(   aoi_glmm_fit,   specs = "aoi_glmm_condition",   by = "aoi_glmm_window",   model_name = "aoi_window_glmm",   type = "response" )  aoi_emmeans$overview aoi_emmeans$emmeans aoi_emmeans$contrasts model_table_files <- export_gazepoint_model_tables(   model_summary = aoi_model_summary,   emmeans_summary = aoi_emmeans,   output_dir = output_dir,   prefix = "aoi_window_glmm" )  model_table_files reporting <- create_gazepoint_reporting_checklist(   data = master,   objects = list(     validation = validation,     aoi_geometry_audit = aoi_geometry_audit,     aoi_denominator_audit = aoi_window_denominator_audit,     aoi_model = aoi_glmm_fit,     diagnostics = aoi_diagnostics   ),   analysis_type = "aoi",   study_title = "Gazepoint AOI study" )  reporting$overview reporting$checklist

title: Cluster-permutation time-course workflow
code: set.seed(123)  raw <- simulate_gazepoint_cluster_timecourse_data(   n_subjects = 12,   n_time_bins = 30,   conditions = c("control", "treatment"),   effect_start = 12,   effect_end = 20,   effect_size = 0.7,   seed = 123 )  head(raw) #>   subject condition time_bin      outcome #> 1    S001   control        1  0.023352640 #> 2    S002   control        1  0.006406593 #> 3    S003   control        1  0.276462794 #> 4    S004   control        1  0.767104526 #> 5    S005   control        1  0.269113265 #> 6    S006   control        1 -0.240940613

title: Cluster-permutation time-course workflow
text: prepare_gazepoint_timecourse_test_data() converts long-format data set internal column contract expected run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation().
code: prepared <- prepare_gazepoint_timecourse_test_data(   raw,   subject_col = "subject",   condition_col = "condition",   time_col = "time_bin",   outcome_col = "outcome",   condition_order = c("control", "treatment") )  head(prepared) #>   .gp3_cluster_subject .gp3_cluster_condition .gp3_cluster_time_bin #> 1                 S001                control                     1 #> 2                 S001              treatment                     1 #> 3                 S001                control                     2 #> 4                 S001              treatment                     2 #> 5                 S001                control                     3 #> 6                 S001              treatment                     3 #>   .gp3_cluster_outcome .gp3_cluster_status #> 1           0.02335264                  ok #> 2          -0.38697165                  ok #> 3           0.11443486                  ok #> 4           0.20485385                  ok #> 5           0.07188089                  ok #> 6           0.32232050                  ok

title: Cluster-permutation time-course workflow
text: running cluster-permutation workflow, check whether data grid complete, paired, compatible validated two-condition workflow.
code: grid_audit <- audit_gazepoint_timecourse_grid(prepared)  grid_audit$grid_summary #>   n_input_rows n_valid_rows n_subjects n_conditions n_time_bins #> 1          720          720         12            2          30 #>   n_expected_cells n_observed_cells n_missing_cells n_duplicate_cells #> 1              720              720               0                 0 #>   n_unpaired_subject_time_cells #> 1                             0 grid_audit$readiness #>                            check passed #> 1         exactly_two_conditions   TRUE #> 2          no_missing_grid_cells   TRUE #> 3             no_duplicate_cells   TRUE #> 4 no_unpaired_subject_time_cells   TRUE #> 5                numeric_outcome   TRUE design_diagnostic <- diagnose_gazepoint_cluster_design(prepared)  design_diagnostic #>         diagnostic                                                    value #> 1       conditions                                                        2 #> 2 paired_time_grid                                                        0 #> 3       duplicates                                                        0 #> 4      sample_size                                                       12 #> 5        time_bins                                                       30 #> 6  validated_scope two-condition within-subject one-dimensional time course #>   passed #> 1   TRUE #> 2   TRUE #> 3   TRUE #> 4   TRUE #> 5   TRUE #> 6   TRUE #>                                                                                                                         interpretation #> 1                                                                    The implemented inferential engine is for exactly two conditions. #> 2                                                                      Each subject-time cell should have both conditions represented. #> 3                                                                     Duplicate cells should be aggregated before permutation testing. #> 4                                                                         Permutation stability depends on the number of paired units. #> 5                                                                     Cluster formation requires an ordered one-dimensional time grid. #> 6 ANOVA, mixed-model, TFCE, multidimensional, covariate-adjusted, and parallel engines are intentionally outside this validated scope.

title: Cluster-permutation time-course workflow
text: fast documentation example, article uses small number permutations. Real analyses use larger number report settings.
code: cluster_result <- run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(   prepared,   condition_order = c("control", "treatment"),   n_permutations = 99,   cluster_threshold = 1.5,   min_time_bins = 2,   seed = 123 )  names(cluster_result) #> [1] "timecourse"               "clusters"                 #> [3] "permutation_distribution" "data"                     #> [5] "settings"                 "model_status"             #> [7] "n_subjects"               "n_time_bins"              #> [9] "warning"

title: Cluster-permutation time-course workflow
code: cluster_summary <- summarize_gazepoint_time_clusters(cluster_result)  cluster_summary #>   cluster_id cluster_direction start_time_bin end_time_bin n_time_bins #> 1          1          positive             12           20           9 #>   cluster_statistic p_value cluster_significant cluster_summary_status #> 1          42.47172    0.01                TRUE                     ok

title: Cluster-permutation time-course workflow
code: plot_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(   cluster_result,   plot_type = "difference",   significant_only = FALSE )

title: Cluster-permutation time-course workflow
text: report_gazepoint_cluster_permutation() returns compact report object. generated wording deliberately cautious: detected ranges treated cluster-level time intervals, exact effect-onset effect-offset estimates.
code: cluster_report <- report_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(cluster_result)  cluster_report$report_text #> [1] "The cluster-permutation workflow identified 1 cluster(s) below alpha = 0.05 over the following time-bin range(s): 12-20 (p = 0.01) . These ranges should be reported as cluster-level time intervals, not as precise effect-onset or effect-offset estimates."

title: Cluster-permutation time-course workflow
text: Cluster-level results can depend cluster-forming threshold. compact sensitivity table helps document whether broad pattern stable across reasonable threshold choices.
code: threshold_sensitivity <- run_gazepoint_cluster_threshold_sensitivity(   prepared,   thresholds = c(1.25, 1.5, 1.75),   condition_order = c("control", "treatment"),   n_permutations = 49,   min_time_bins = 2,   seed = 123 )  threshold_sensitivity$summary #>   cluster_threshold n_clusters n_significant_clusters min_p_value #> 1              1.25          1                      1        0.02 #> 2              1.50          1                      1        0.02 #> 3              1.75          1                      1        0.02

title: Heatmaps and spatial visualisation
text: plot_gazepoint_heatmap_overlay() can place heatmap PNG stimulus image. background-image helper uses optional png package.
code: if (requireNamespace("png", quietly = TRUE)) {   bg <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")    img <- array(1, dim = c(720, 1280, 3))   img[150:570, 120:520, ] <- 0.92   img[220:520, 720:1120, ] <- 0.86    png::writePNG(img, bg)    plot_gazepoint_heatmap_overlay(     gaze,     background_image = bg,     x_col = "x",     y_col = "y",     weight_col = "duration",     display_width = 1280,     display_height = 720,     bins = 45,     heatmap_alpha = 0.70   ) }

title: Missingness and data-coverage reporting
text: summarize_gazepoint_missingness() computes missing observed rates selected variables, optionally within grouping columns. British spelling alias also available.
code: missingness <- summarize_gazepoint_missingness(   synthetic,   cols = c("pupil_left", "pupil_right", "pupil", "gaze_x", "gaze_y"),   group_cols = "condition" )  missingness #>     group_id    variable n_rows n_missing n_observed missing_rate observed_rate #> 1    control  pupil_left    160        13        147      0.08125       0.91875 #> 2    control pupil_right    160        13        147      0.08125       0.91875 #> 3    control       pupil    160        13        147      0.08125       0.91875 #> 4    control      gaze_x    160         0        160      0.00000       1.00000 #> 5    control      gaze_y    160         0        160      0.00000       1.00000 #> 6  treatment  pupil_left    160        17        143      0.10625       0.89375 #> 7  treatment pupil_right    160        17        143      0.10625       0.89375 #> 8  treatment       pupil    160        17        143      0.10625       0.89375 #> 9  treatment      gaze_x    160         0        160      0.00000       1.00000 #> 10 treatment      gaze_y    160         0        160      0.00000       1.00000 summarise_gazepoint_missingness(   synthetic,   cols = c("pupil_left", "pupil_right", "pupil") ) #>   group_id    variable n_rows n_missing n_observed missing_rate observed_rate #> 1      all  pupil_left    320        30        290      0.09375       0.90625 #> 2      all pupil_right    320        30        290      0.09375       0.90625 #> 3      all       pupil    320        30        290      0.09375       0.90625

title: Missingness and data-coverage reporting
text: plot_gazepoint_missingness_profile() can use either raw data missingness summary.  tile view can useful many groups variables.
code: plot_gazepoint_missingness_profile(   missingness,   plot_type = "bar",   title = "Missingness by condition and variable" ) plot_gazepoint_missingness_profile(   missingness,   plot_type = "tile",   title = "Missingness profile" )

title: Missingness and data-coverage reporting
text: report_gazepoint_missingness() returns summary table, overall table, variable-level table, compact report text. generated wording deliberately cautious. Missingness rates data-coverage diagnostics interpreted together study design, preprocessing decisions, planned exclusion criteria.
code: missingness_report <- report_gazepoint_missingness(   missingness,   digits = 1,   max_variables = 3 )  missingness_report$overall #>   n_variables n_groups total_cells total_missing overall_missing_rate #> 1           5        2        1600            90              0.05625 missingness_report$variable_summary #>      variable n_rows n_missing missing_rate #> 3       pupil    320        30      0.09375 #> 4  pupil_left    320        30      0.09375 #> 5 pupil_right    320        30      0.09375 #> 1      gaze_x    320         0      0.00000 #> 2      gaze_y    320         0      0.00000 missingness_report$report_text #> [1] "Missingness was summarized across 5 variable(s). The overall cell-level missingness rate was 5.6%. The highest missingness variable(s) were: pupil (9.4%), pupil_left (9.4%), pupil_right (9.4%). These values are descriptive data-coverage diagnostics and do not by themselves define exclusion decisions."

title: Pupil preprocessing and analysis workflow
text: vignette shows practical pupil workflow Gazepoint GP3 / Gazepoint Analysis exports using gp3tools. workflow covers: creating master table; running light pupil preprocessing branch; running conservative artifact-cleaned pupil branch; auditing pupil preprocessing quality; summarising pupil windows; fitting confirmatory pupil-window models; running time-course models sensitivity checks. vignette chunks evaluated package checks, file can serve readable workflow template. first section uses built-example data; optional import chunk shows replace objects data private Gazepoint export folder. adapting code, replace placeholder paths column names used study.

title: Pupil preprocessing and analysis workflow
text: quick read-, start lightweight synthetic example data included package. objects make later workflow easier follow already use column names expected examples. real study, replace example objects importing Gazepoint export folder. optional chunk creates all_gaze rebuilds master imported rows:
code: library(gp3tools)  data("gazepoint_example_master") data("gazepoint_example_pupil_windows")  master <- gazepoint_example_master pupil_windows <- gazepoint_example_pupil_windows export_dir <- "C:/Users/YourName/Desktop/gp3_test_exports" output_dir <- "C:/Users/YourName/Desktop/gp3_outputs"  results <- run_gazepoint_workflow(   export_dir = export_dir,   output_dir = output_dir,   prefix = "study1",   save_plots = TRUE,   create_report = TRUE )  all_gaze <- results$all_gaze master <- create_gazepoint_master(all_gaze)

code: master_audit <- audit_gazepoint_master(master)  validation <- validate_gazepoint_master(master)  master_audit$overview validation$summary validation$checks

title: Pupil preprocessing and analysis workflow
text: Use light branch need transparent minimal preprocessing pipeline. Create pupil-window summaries. summaries useful confirmatory window analyses, time window specified testing main condition effect.
code: pupil_summary <- summarise_gazepoint_pupil(master)  flagged_pupil <- flag_gazepoint_pupil(   master,   pupil_col = "pupil" )  interpolated_pupil <- interpolate_gazepoint_pupil(   flagged_pupil,   pupil_col = "pupil_for_preprocessing",   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global") )  baseline_corrected <- baseline_correct_gazepoint_pupil(   interpolated_pupil,   pupil_col = "pupil_interpolated",   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   baseline_window = c(0, 200),   min_baseline_samples = 1 )  smoothed_pupil <- smooth_gazepoint_pupil(   baseline_corrected,   pupil_col = "pupil_baseline_corrected",   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   window_samples = 5,   method = "mean",   align = "center",   min_points = 2 ) pupil_windows <- summarise_gazepoint_pupil_windows(   smoothed_pupil,   pupil_col = "pupil_smoothed",   time_col = "time",   windows = c(0, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000),   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global", "condition"),   min_valid_samples = 1 )  pupil_windows

title: Pupil preprocessing and analysis workflow
text: Use conservative branch blink/trackloss padding, pupil-speed artifacts, stricter preprocessing decisions important.
code: registry <- create_gazepoint_preprocessing_registry(   blink_padding_ms = 50,   interpolation_max_gap_ms = 150,   smoothing_window_samples = 5,   baseline_window = c(0, 200) )  artifact_pupil <- flag_gazepoint_pupil_artifacts(   master,   registry = registry,   pupil_col = "pupil",   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global") )  interpolated_artifact_pupil <- interpolate_gazepoint_pupil(   artifact_pupil,   pupil_col = "pupil_clean",   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global") )  baseline_artifact_pupil <- baseline_correct_gazepoint_pupil(   interpolated_artifact_pupil,   pupil_col = "pupil_interpolated",   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   baseline_window = c(0, 200),   min_baseline_samples = 1 )  smoothed_artifact_pupil <- smooth_gazepoint_pupil(   baseline_artifact_pupil,   pupil_col = "pupil_baseline_corrected",   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global"),   window_samples = 5,   method = "mean",   align = "center",   min_points = 2 )  pupil_windows_conservative <- summarise_gazepoint_pupil_windows(   smoothed_artifact_pupil,   pupil_col = "pupil_smoothed",   time_col = "time",   windows = c(0, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000),   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global", "condition"),   min_valid_samples = 1 )

title: Pupil preprocessing and analysis workflow
code: gap_audit <- audit_gazepoint_pupil_gaps(   interpolated_artifact_pupil,   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global") )  baseline_audit <- audit_gazepoint_pupil_baseline(   baseline_artifact_pupil,   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global") )  imbalance_audit <- audit_gazepoint_pupil_imbalance(   smoothed_artifact_pupil,   group_cols = "condition" )  drift_audit <- audit_gazepoint_pupil_drift(   smoothed_artifact_pupil,   pupil_col = "pupil_smoothed",   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global") )  overlap_audit <- audit_gazepoint_pupil_overlap_risk(   master,   time_col = "time",   event_col = "event_label",   group_cols = c("subject", "MEDIA_ID", "trial_global") )  gap_audit$overview baseline_audit$overview imbalance_audit$overview drift_audit$overview overlap_audit$overview

title: Pupil preprocessing and analysis workflow
text: Prepare pupil-window model data: Fit confirmatory model: Run sensitivity checks:
code: pupil_window_model_data <- prepare_gazepoint_pupil_window_model_data(   pupil_windows_conservative,   outcome_col = "mean_pupil",   subject_col = "subject",   condition_col = "condition",   window_col = "window_label",   trial_col = "trial_global",   valid_samples_col = "n_valid_samples",   total_samples_col = "n_samples",   min_valid_samples = 1 )  dplyr::count(   pupil_window_model_data,   pupil_window_condition,   pupil_window_label,   pupil_window_model_status ) pupil_window_lmm <- fit_gazepoint_pupil_window_lmm(   pupil_window_model_data,   random_window_slopes = FALSE,   use_weights = TRUE,   REML = FALSE )  pupil_window_lmm$model_status pupil_window_lmm$formula pupil_window_lmm$fixed_effects pupil_window_lmm$comparison pupil_window_sensitivity <- fit_gazepoint_pupil_window_sensitivity(   pupil_window_model_data,   model_types = c(     "unweighted_lmm",     "weighted_lmm",     "fixed_lm",     "weighted_lm"   ),   include_condition = TRUE,   include_window = TRUE,   include_interaction = TRUE )  pupil_window_sensitivity$comparison pupil_window_sensitivity$fixed_effects

title: Pupil preprocessing and analysis workflow
text: Prepare binned pupil time-course data: Fit main pupil GAMM: Run gaze-position/PFE sensitivity GAMM: Prepare fit Growth Curve Analysis model:
code: pupil_gamm_data <- prepare_gazepoint_pupil_gamm_data(   smoothed_artifact_pupil,   pupil_col = "pupil_smoothed",   time_col = "time",   subject_col = "subject",   condition_col = "condition",   trial_col = "trial_global",   bin_width_ms = 50,   min_valid_samples = 1 ) pupil_gamm_fit <- fit_gazepoint_pupil_gamm(   pupil_gamm_data,   n_time_basis = 10,   discrete = TRUE )  pupil_gamm_fit$model_status pupil_gamm_fit$formula pupil_pfe_fit <- fit_gazepoint_pupil_pfe_gamm(   pupil_gamm_data,   n_time_basis = 10,   n_position_basis = 8,   discrete = TRUE )  pupil_pfe_fit$sensitivity_status pupil_pfe_fit$comparison pupil_gca_data <- prepare_gazepoint_gca_data(   pupil_gamm_data,   pupil_col = "mean_pupil",   time_col = "time_bin_center_ms",   subject_col = "subject",   condition_col = "condition",   degree = 3,   orthogonal = TRUE,   valid_samples_col = "n_valid_samples",   min_valid_samples = 1 )  pupil_gca_fit <- fit_gazepoint_gca(   pupil_gca_data,   REML = FALSE )  pupil_gca_fit$model_status pupil_gca_fit$comparison

text: Cluster-permutation divergence-point analyses exploratory time-course tools. Estimate earliest reliable divergence point: use detected exploratory cluster define confirmatory window retest window specified priori.
code: cluster_data <- prepare_gazepoint_cluster_data(   pupil_gamm_data,   outcome_col = "mean_pupil",   time_col = "time_bin_center_ms",   subject_col = "subject",   condition_col = "condition" )  cluster_results <- run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(   cluster_data,   condition_levels = c("control", "treatment"),   n_permutations = 1000,   seed = 123 )  cluster_summary <- summarise_gazepoint_clusters(cluster_results)  cluster_summary$overview cluster_summary$significant_clusters divergence <- estimate_gazepoint_divergence_point(   data = pupil_gamm_data,   outcome_col = "mean_pupil",   time_col = "time_bin_center_ms",   condition_col = "condition",   participant_col = "subject",   comparison = c("control", "treatment"),   bootstrap_unit = "participant",   n_boot = 1000,   consecutive_points = 2,   seed = 123 )  divergence$overview divergence$divergence_point

code: pupil_diagnostics <- diagnose_gazepoint_glmm(   pupil_window_lmm,   model_name = "pupil_window_lmm",   use_dharma = FALSE )  pupil_model_summary <- tidy_gazepoint_model_summary(   pupil_window_lmm,   model_name = "pupil_window_lmm" )  pupil_diagnostics$overview pupil_model_summary$fixed_effects reporting <- create_gazepoint_reporting_checklist(   data = master,   objects = list(     validation = validation,     gap_audit = gap_audit,     baseline_audit = baseline_audit,     pupil_model = pupil_window_lmm,     diagnostics = pupil_diagnostics   ),   analysis_type = "pupil",   study_title = "Gazepoint pupil study" )  reporting$overview reporting$checklist

title: Statistical extensions and plots
code: set.seed(2026)  aoi_demo <- expand.grid(   subject_id = paste0('S', sprintf('%02d', 1:6)),   trial_id = paste0('T', 1:3),   time_ms = seq(0, 1200, by = 100),   KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  aoi_demo$condition <- ifelse(aoi_demo$subject_id %in% paste0('S', sprintf('%02d', 1:3)), 'control', 'treatment') aoi_demo$aoi <- sample(c('Claim', 'Evidence', 'CTA', 'Navigation'), nrow(aoi_demo), replace = TRUE)  fix_demo <- data.frame(   subject_id = 'S01',   trial_id = 'T1',   fixation_index = 1:8,   start_time_ms = seq(0, 1050, by = 150),   end_time_ms = seq(120, 1170, by = 150),   x = c(220, 260, 310, 470, 520, 610, 700, 760),   y = c(180, 210, 240, 260, 330, 360, 390, 420),   aoi = c('Claim', 'Claim', 'Evidence', 'Evidence', 'CTA', 'CTA', 'Navigation', 'Navigation'),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  effect_demo <- data.frame(   time_ms = seq(0, 1200, by = 100),   estimate = seq(0.18, 0.42, length.out = 13) + sin(seq(0, pi, length.out = 13)) * 0.05 ) effect_demo$lower <- pmax(0, effect_demo$estimate - 0.07) effect_demo$upper <- pmin(1, effect_demo$estimate + 0.07)  model_demo <- data.frame(   target_prop = c(0.20, 0.24, 0.28, 0.31, 0.35, 0.39, 0.43, 0.48),   condition = rep(c('control', 'treatment'), each = 4) ) model_fit <- stats::lm(target_prop ~ condition, data = model_demo)

what: Import, Inspect, Analyse, and Report Gazepoint GP3 Exports
title: Import, Inspect, Analyse, and Report Gazepoint GP3 Exports
text: gp3tools provides tools importing, checking, summarising, exporting, visualising, preprocessing, modelling Gazepoint GP3 / Gazepoint Analysis export files R. package designed exported Gazepoint CSV files, especially: User *_all_gaze.csv User *_fixations.csv Data_Summary_export_*.csv supports common Gazepoint workflows, including: folder-level import one-command workflow execution; sampling-rate checks tracking-quality summaries; sample-level master-table creation, auditing, validation; light conservative pupil preprocessing; pupil preprocessing audits, reliability checks, interpolation sensitivity, stimulus-luminance auditing; pupil-window confirmatory LMMs model-family sensitivity checks; AOI entries, AOI windows, AOI denominators, AOI-window GLMMs; AOI/fixation/transition feature extraction time-varying transition matrices; fixation-, saccade-, AOI-contingent alignment; pupil GAMMs, AOI GAMMs, gaze-position/PFE sensitivity GAMMs, Growth Curve Analysis; cluster-based permutation tests bootstrapped divergence-point estimation; standalone model diagnostics GLMM/LMM/GLM GAM/BAM models; nested model comparison, model-implied prediction plotting, leave-one-unit model sensitivity; manuscript-ready fixed-effect, EMM, contrast, diagnostic, model-export tables; real-data readiness gates, reporting checklists, final analysis-decision audits; explicit trial participant exclusion recommendations; offline gaze recalibration / drift correction known target coordinates available; diagnostic plots, lightweight HTML reports, CSV exports; package-adapter exports eyetrackingR-style, pupillometryR-style, gazer-style, eyetools-style workflows; optional external gazeR eyetools cross-check workflows.

title: Import, Inspect, Analyse, and Report Gazepoint GP3 Exports
text: Use workflow matches research question stage analysis. quick first pass exported Gazepoint files, use run_gazepoint_workflow(). imports folder, checks file pairing, summarises sampling tracking quality, creates AOI summaries, writes CSV outputs, optionally saves diagnostic plots HTML report. analysis-ready sample-level data, create master table create_gazepoint_master(), audit audit_gazepoint_master(), validate validate_gazepoint_master(). pupil preprocessing, use light branch need transparent minimal pipeline: Use conservative artifact-cleaned branch blink/trackloss padding, pupil-speed artifacts, binocular disagreement, stricter preprocessing decisions important: Use pupil-window LMMs main hypothesis concerns predefined pupil windows. Use pupil GAMMs, PFE-GAMMs, GCA research question concerns time-course shape. Use AOI-window GLMMs main hypothesis concerns predefined AOI time windows. Use AOI GAMMs research question concerns smooth target-looking trajectories time. Use AOI-entry, fixation, transition helpers analysis concerns looking episodes, fixation summaries, AOI sequences, transition matrices, scanpath structure. Use cluster-based permutation testing time-course inference. Use estimate_gazepoint_divergence_point() complementary onset/sensitivity evidence, replacement confirmatory model specification. Use model diagnostics, model summaries, estimated marginal means, nested model comparison, model-prediction plots, leave-one-unit sensitivity checks manuscript reporting. Use check_gazepoint_real_data_readiness(), recommend_gazepoint_exclusions(), create_gazepoint_reporting_checklist() final interpretation.

code: devtools::install(reload = FALSE) library(gp3tools) packageVersion("gp3tools")

title: Import, Inspect, Analyse, and Report Gazepoint GP3 Exports
text: Use workflow want quick reproducible pass folder Gazepoint exports quality summaries, AOI summaries, exported CSV files, diagnostic plots, optional HTML report. workflow expects exported Gazepoint files : Set folder containing Gazepoint exports output folder results: Run complete folder workflow: returned object named list containing imported files, quality summaries, AOI summaries, exported file paths, saved plot paths, optional report path: Create compact one-row summary workflow: workflow summary useful quickly checking: output_dir provided, workflow writes standard CSV files : save_plots = TRUE, workflow also saves diagnostic plots : create_report = TRUE, workflow creates lightweight HTML report.
code: User 1_all_gaze.csv User 1_fixations.csv Data_Summary_export_*.csv export_dir <- "C:/Users/YourName/Desktop/gp3_test_exports" output_dir <- "C:/Users/YourName/Desktop/gp3_outputs" library(gp3tools)  results <- run_gazepoint_workflow(   export_dir = export_dir,   output_dir = output_dir,   prefix = "study1",   save_plots = TRUE,   create_report = TRUE ) names(results)  results$all_gaze results$all_fix results$sampling results$quality results$flagged_quality results$aoi_table results$written_files results$written_plots results$report_file workflow_summary <- summarise_gazepoint_workflow(results)  print(workflow_summary, width = Inf) workflow_summary$n_all_gaze_rows workflow_summary$n_all_fix_rows workflow_summary$n_sampling_rows workflow_summary$n_quality_rows workflow_summary$n_aoi_rows workflow_summary$n_flagged_recordings workflow_summary$n_written_files workflow_summary$n_written_plots workflow_summary$report_created study1_sampling.csv study1_quality.csv study1_flagged_quality.csv study1_aoi_table.csv study1_tracking_quality_plot.png study1_sampling_rate_plot.png

what: Manual version of the same workflow
title: Import, Inspect, Analyse, and Report Gazepoint GP3 Exports
text: workflow can run step step want control. Read -gaze files: Read fixation files: Check -gaze fixation exports correctly paired: Estimate sampling rate participant stimulus: Gazepoint GP3 60 Hz exports, estimated frequency usually close 60 Hz. Summarise tracking quality: Flag recordings requiring review: Show recordings require review: Create basic AOI summary -gaze fixation exports: AOI table includes one row per user, media stimulus, AOI, metrics : sample-level time first view; sample count; approximate sample-based viewed time; fixation count; total fixation duration; mean fixation duration; fixation-based time first fixation. Write standard output tables: Create standard diagnostic plots: Save standard diagnostic plots: basic workflow useful first-pass inspection. advanced preprocessing modelling, continue creating master table.
code: all_gaze <- read_gazepoint_folder(   export_dir,   pattern = "_all_gaze\\.csv$" )  dim(all_gaze) all_fix <- read_gazepoint_folder(   export_dir,   pattern = "_fixations\\.csv$" )  dim(all_fix) file_pairs <- check_gazepoint_file_pairs(export_dir)  file_pairs sampling <- check_sampling_rate(   all_gaze,   group_cols = c("USER_FILE", "MEDIA_ID") )  sampling quality <- summarise_tracking_quality(   all_gaze,   group_cols = c("USER_FILE", "MEDIA_ID") )  quality flagged_quality <- flag_tracking_quality(   quality = quality,   sampling = sampling,   min_gaze_valid_pct = 70,   min_pupil_valid_pct = 70,   expected_hz = 60,   hz_tolerance = 5 )  flagged_quality dplyr::filter(flagged_quality, review_required) aoi_table <- summarise_gazepoint_aoi(   gaze_data = all_gaze,   fixation_data = all_fix )  aoi_table written_files <- write_gazepoint_outputs(   sampling = sampling,   quality = quality,   flagged_quality = flagged_quality,   aoi_table = aoi_table,   output_dir = output_dir,   prefix = "gazepoint_test" )  written_files plot_tracking_quality(flagged_quality)  plot_sampling_rate(sampling) written_plots <- save_gazepoint_plots(   flagged_quality = flagged_quality,   sampling = sampling,   output_dir = output_dir,   prefix = "gazepoint_test" )  written_plots

title: Import, Inspect, Analyse, and Report Gazepoint GP3 Exports
text: advanced preprocessing, AOI modelling, pupil modelling, time-course analysis, reporting, first convert imported Gazepoint -gaze export standard sample-level master table. Start -gaze data imported run_gazepoint_workflow() read_gazepoint_folder(): Create master table: master table standardises key fields available, including: participant ID; media/stimulus ID; trial identifiers; time; gaze coordinates; pupil values; gaze-validity pupil-validity fields; AOI state; fixation-related fields; event labels; response-related columns; screen metadata fields. Inspect main columns: Audit master table: Use audit check whether imported data contain expected participants, stimuli, AOI states, pupil values, gaze-coordinate availability. Validate master table preprocessing modelling: Validation formal gate. Use pupil preprocessing, AOI modelling, time-course analysis, final reporting. Export master table audit outputs: writes master table, audit tables, validation outputs CSV files.
code: all_gaze <- results$all_gaze master <- create_gazepoint_master(all_gaze)  dplyr::glimpse(master) names(master)  dplyr::glimpse(   dplyr::select(     master,     dplyr::any_of(c(       "subject",       "MEDIA_ID",       "trial_global",       "time",       "x",       "y",       "pupil",       "aoi_current",       "event_label"     ))   ) ) master_audit <- audit_gazepoint_master(master)  master_audit$overview master_audit$subject_summary master_audit$media_summary master_audit$aoi_summary master_audit$pupil_summary master_audit$coordinate_summary validation <- validate_gazepoint_master(master)  validation$summary validation$checks master_outputs <- export_gazepoint_master_audit(   master,   output_dir = output_dir,   prefix = "study1_master" )  master_outputs

Metrics recomputed -gaze fixation files may always exactly reproduce Gazepoint’s internal calculations. transparent reproducible analysis exported rows, continue sample-level master table preprocessing/modelling helpers.

title: Import, Inspect, Analyse, and Report Gazepoint GP3 Exports
text: Use light branch need transparent, minimal pupil preprocessing pipeline. branch appropriate early inspection, teaching examples, studies conservative artifact padding required. First summarise pupil availability: Flag missing, non-finite, implausible, IQR-outlying pupil samples: Interpolate short internal pupil gaps: Apply baseline correction: Smooth baseline-corrected pupil signal: Summarise processed pupil signal analysis windows: light branch gives simple path raw pupil values window-level summaries. stricter cleaning, use conservative artifact-cleaned branch.

"auto" detects normalised coordinates coordinate values mostly 0 1. event_latency_offset_ms Optional timing correction milliseconds. Positive values shift event/sample time forward.

Defaults "mean". min_baseline_samples Minimum number valid baseline samples required compute baseline. Defaults 1.

Defaults 50. baseline_start_ms Baseline-window start, milliseconds. Defaults -200. baseline_end_ms Baseline-window end, milliseconds. Defaults 6. baseline_missing_prop_threshold Baseline missingness threshold used baseline-quality audits. Defaults 0.30. baseline_interpolated_prop_threshold Baseline interpolation threshold used baseline-quality audits. Defaults 0.30. baseline_artifact_prop_threshold Baseline artifact threshold used baseline-quality audits. Defaults 0.30. overlap_trial_duration_ms Trial-duration threshold pupil overlap/deconvolution risk considered. Defaults 3000. overlap_event_gap_ms Event-gap threshold pupil-response overlap considered. Defaults 1000.

title: Estimate a bootstrapped divergence point between two Gazepoint time courses — estimate_gazepoint_divergence_point
code: estimate_gazepoint_divergence_point(   data,   outcome_col,   time_col,   condition_col,   participant_col = NULL,   trial_col = NULL,   comparison = NULL,   bootstrap_unit = c("participant", "trial", "row"),   summary_function = c("mean", "median"),   n_boot = 1000L,   ci = 0.95,   consecutive_points = 1L,   null_value = 0,   min_abs_difference = 0,   direction = c("two_sided", "positive", "negative"),   seed = NULL,   keep_bootstrap = TRUE,   name = "gazepoint_divergence_point" )

title: Estimate a bootstrapped divergence point between two Gazepoint time courses — estimate_gazepoint_divergence_point
text: data data frame containing time-course observations. outcome_col Outcome column, example pupil size, fixation probability, gaze proportion, AOI time-course value. time_col Time column. condition_col Condition column. Exactly two conditions compared unless comparison supplied. participant_col Optional participant column used participant-level bootstrap resampling. trial_col Optional trial column used trial-level bootstrap resampling. comparison Optional character vector two condition values. estimated difference comparison[2] - comparison[1]. bootstrap_unit Resampling unit. Options "participant", "trial", "row". summary_function Function used summarise observations within condition--time cells. Options "mean" "median". n_boot Number bootstrap resamples. ci Confidence level bootstrap intervals. consecutive_points Number consecutive time points required declaring divergence. null_value Null difference value. Default 0. min_abs_difference Optional minimum absolute observed difference required time point. direction Direction divergence. "two_sided" checks whether bootstrap interval excludes null_value either direction. "positive" checks whether comparison[2] > comparison[1]. "negative" checks whether comparison[2] < comparison[1]. seed Optional random seed reproducible bootstrap resampling. keep_bootstrap Logical. TRUE, return bootstrap differences time point. name Character label stored returned object.

title: Interpolate short missing gaps in Gazepoint pupil data — interpolate_gazepoint_pupil

NULL, function detects one pupil_clean, pupil_for_preprocessing, mean_pupil, pupil, pupil_raw, left_pupil, right_pupil. time_col Optional name time column. NULL, function detects one time_ms, time, time_orig, time_orig_ms. group_cols Character vector grouping columns used keep interpolation within independent time series. Defaults c("subject", "media_id") using internally standardised names. Use character(0) global interpolation. max_gap_ms Maximum duration, milliseconds, gap may interpolated. duration measured valid samples immediately gap. Defaults 150. max_gap_samples Maximum number consecutive missing samples may interpolated. Defaults Inf. min_valid_points Minimum number valid samples required within group interpolation attempted. Defaults 2.

Required outcome_col = NULL. outcome_col Optional logical 0/1 numeric column indicating target AOI looking sample level. supplied, takes priority aoi_col target_aoi_values. subject_col Name subject/participant column. condition_col Name condition column. unavailable, single fallback condition created. time_col Name time column, milliseconds. trial_col Optional trial identifier column. time_bin_col Optional existing time-bin column. TRUE, bins zero low denominators removed returned data. missing_condition_label Fallback condition label usable condition column available. outcome_label Descriptive label AOI-GAMM outcome.

what: Run paired cluster-based permutation tests — run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation
title: Run paired cluster-based permutation tests — run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation
text: Run paired cluster-based permutation test time-course data prepared prepare_gazepoint_cluster_data(). function tests whether two conditions diverge time controlling cluster-level inference using permutation distribution maximum cluster statistics.

title: Run paired cluster-based permutation tests — run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation
code: run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation(   data,   condition_order = NULL,   n_permutations = 1000,   cluster_threshold = 2,   tail = c("two_sided", "greater", "less"),   cluster_stat = c("sum_abs_t", "sum_t", "size"),   min_time_bins = 1,   seed = NULL,   paired = TRUE )

title: Run paired cluster-based permutation tests — run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation
text: data Cluster-ready data produced prepare_gazepoint_cluster_data(). condition_order Optional character vector length 2 defining two conditions order. tested difference condition 2 minus condition 1. n_permutations Number sign-flip permutations. cluster_threshold Absolute t-statistic threshold forming candidate clusters. tail = "greater" tail = "less", positive threshold used requested direction. tail Direction test. "two_sided" tests positive negative clusters. "greater" tests condition 2 greater condition 1. "less" tests condition 2 less condition 1. cluster_stat Cluster statistic. "sum_abs_t" sums absolute t-statistics within cluster. "sum_t" sums signed t-statistics uses absolute value cluster-level inference. "size" uses number time bins. min_time_bins Minimum number adjacent time bins required cluster retained. seed Optional random seed reproducible permutations. paired Logical. Currently paired within-subject sign-flip permutation supported.

title: Run paired cluster-based permutation tests — run_gazepoint_cluster_permutation

Defaults 50. max_offscreen_gaze_pct Maximum acceptable percentage -screen gaze samples. Defaults 25. require_pupil Logical. TRUE, validation fails usable pupil column present. Defaults FALSE. require_aoi Logical. TRUE, validation fails real AOI samples present. Defaults FALSE. fail_on_error Logical. TRUE, function aborts one validation checks fail. Defaults FALSE.

text: Added summarise_gazepoint_pupil() first pupil-preprocessing gate Gazepoint master tables. function summarises pupil availability, missing-pupil percentages, valid-pupil percentages, pupil distributions, plausible-value checks, IQR-based outlier counts subject, media, subject--media, overall. Added create_gazepoint_preprocessing_registry() storing reusable preprocessing parameters, including blink/artifact padding, interpolation gap thresholds, smoothing window size, baseline windows, physiological pupil thresholds, speed-outlier thresholds, binocular-disagreement thresholds, baseline-quality thresholds, overlap-risk settings. Added flag_gazepoint_pupil_artifacts() conservative pupil artifact cleaning interpolation. function flags blink/trackloss contamination, missing pupil samples, non-finite non-positive values, pupil-speed outliers, binocular left-right pupil disagreement, temporal padding around bad samples. also includes scale-safety rule suppresses millimetre-based physiological thresholds remove nearly non-missing samples, protects Gazepoint raw-unit exports silently erased. Added interpolate_gazepoint_pupil() linearly interpolating short internal gaps pupil time series. function automatically prefers pupil_clean available, followed pupil_for_preprocessing, preserves leading/trailing gaps, avoids long gaps, respects grouping subject/media/trial, records interpolation status, gap size, gap duration, produces pupil_interpolated later filtering baseline correction. Added baseline_correct_gazepoint_pupil() flexible baseline correction Gazepoint pupil data flagging interpolation. function supports window-based baselines c(-200, 0) c(0, 200), well user-defined logical baseline/pre-stimulus flag columns. produces absolute baseline-corrected values, percent change, ratio, z-scored baseline correction, baseline availability flags, baseline sample counts, baseline-status labels. Added smooth_gazepoint_pupil() sample-based rolling smoothing Gazepoint pupil time series interpolation optional baseline correction. function supports mean median smoothing, centred/right/left-aligned windows, custom grouping subject/media/trial columns, optional preservation missing input rows, records smoothing status, window size, input column, method, alignment, minimum valid-points settings. Added summarise_gazepoint_pupil_windows() aggregating processed Gazepoint pupil data user-defined analysis windows. function supports numeric window breakpoints labelled custom window tables, flexible grouping subject, media, trial, condition, AOI, columns, produces analysis-ready summaries including valid/missing pupil counts, percentages, mean, median, SD, quantiles, min/max, AUC, time span, window-validity status.

Added complete preprocessing multiverse branch defining, running, summarising, plotting pupil AOI preprocessing sensitivity checks across alternative preprocessing decisions. Expanded sensitivity layer beyond model-family sensitivity checks supporting preprocessing-decision multiverses pupil interpolation, smoothing, baseline windows, artifact padding, AOI denominator definitions, minimum denominator thresholds. Added tests preprocessing multiverse specification, pupil multiverse runners, AOI multiverse runners, multiverse summaries, multiverse plots. Added complete general publication-level audit branch checking event synchronisation, design balance, exclusion flow, gaze-signal quality, condition-level quality imbalance, post-exclusion retained-sample balance. Added complete AOI geometry verification branch checking AOI geometry validity, AOI overlap, margin sensitivity, observed-versus-derived AOI coding consistency, visual AOI verification. Added check_gazepoint_real_data_readiness(), explicit final readiness gate real-data analysis. helper returns structured overview, gate_decision, checks, detected_columns, data_summary, condition_summary, settings outputs, pass/warn/fail readiness status. Added run_gazepoint_eyetools_fixation_detection(), optional external-detector wrapper eyetools. wrapper prepares Gazepoint data using expected pID, trial, time, x, y schema, supports dispersion, VTI fixation, VTI saccade branches, records clean skipped, complete, partial-complete, error statuses. Added create_gazepoint_reporting_checklist(), auto-generated reporting checklist manuscript/report preparation. summarises reporting readiness across data structure, readiness gates, import/workflow checks, sampling/tracking quality, AOI reporting, pupil reporting, model diagnostics, sensitivity analyses, reproducibility, optional advanced methods. Added compute_gazepoint_time_varying_transition_matrix(), dedicated helper transition-count transition-probability matrices time window grouping variables. Added fit_gazepoint_transition_count_nb_sensitivity(), optional negative-binomial transition-count sensitivity model using glmmTMB available. Added run_gazepoint_gazer_crosscheck(), optional external gazeR preprocessing cross-check wrapper. Added audit_gazepoint_stimulus_luminance(), audit_gazepoint_pupil_reliability(), interpolate_gazepoint_pupil_pchip() pupil-analysis robustness reporting support. Added advanced validation tests bootstrapped divergence-point analysis, leave-one-unit model sensitivity, fixation/saccade-contingent alignment, AOI empirical-logit transformation, model-prediction plotting, nested model comparison, Hampel pupil artifact detection, offline gaze recalibration, explicit trial/participant exclusion recommendations. Resolved R CMD check notes caused .env pronoun use internal helper functions replacing dplyr pronoun-based assignment explicit local data-frame assignment.
